league.name: Série A
league.description: Brazil's Campeonato Brasileiro Série A — flair, clássicos, Libertadores places, and one of the world's deepest talent pools.
league.styleOfPlay: Technical, creative, and transition-heavy; wing play and 1v1 skill are central to how fans describe the league.
league.historySummary: Série A runs on promotion/relegation with state championships feeding club identity. Libertadores success shapes how Brazilians rank their clubs.
league.fanGuide: Start with a clássico and one grande club from Rio or São Paulo. Learn why state leagues matter in club history even if you only follow Série A.

teams.name: Chapecoense
teams.shortHistory: Chapecoense is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Chapecoense's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Flamengo
teams.shortHistory: Flamengo are Brazil's best-supported club, with a trophy room built on Libertadores nights and domestic dominance from Rio.
teams.fanGuide: Expect attacking football, enormous away support, and the Fla-Flu derby against Fluminense as the emotional starting point.

teams.name: Botafogo
teams.shortHistory: Botafogo is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Botafogo's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Santos
teams.shortHistory: Santos are the club of Pelé's roots and endless youth talent — the Vila Belmiro factory still defines Brazilian romance with the game.
teams.fanGuide: Peixe culture on the coast; Neymar and Gabigol stories tie the past to the present before you dive into Paulista rivals.

teams.name: São Paulo
teams.shortHistory: São Paulo FC are a three-world-title institution in Brazilian football, known for structure, academies, and big-game pedigree.
teams.fanGuide: Tricolor Paulista identity runs deep — Morumbi nights and clássicos against Corinthians and Palmeiras anchor the learning path.

teams.name: Palmeiras
teams.shortHistory: Palmeiras built a modern winning machine in São Paulo, with repeated Brasileirão titles and strong Libertadores pedigree.
teams.fanGuide: Green-wall atmosphere at Allianz Parque; learn the Derby Paulista against Corinthians before the state-vs-state storylines.

teams.name: Corinthians
teams.shortHistory: Corinthians carry the Fiel torcida identity — working-class roots, huge crowds, and fierce Paulista derbies.
teams.fanGuide: Start with the Derby Paulista vs Palmeiras; the stadium roar matters as much as the tactical shape.

teams.name: Internacional
teams.shortHistory: Internacional is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Internacional's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

players.name: Gustavo Henrique
players.quickFact: Brazil centre-back who captained Corinthians with aerial strength and leadership in the heart of defence.
players.playingStyle: Physical centre-back who defends the box, wins duels, and plays simple passes out from the back.
quizHints: Brazil centre-back in Corinthians black and white.
Tall defender known for headers and organising the back line.
Gustavo Henrique — not Gustavo Gómez of Palmeiras.

players.name: Matheuzinho
players.quickFact: Brazil right-back known for overlapping runs and delivery from wide areas at Corinthians.
players.playingStyle: Attacking full-back who overlaps, delivers driven crosses, and recovers with pace.
quizHints: Brazil full-back who attacks down the right for Corinthians.
Overlapping defender known for pace and crosses.
Matheuzinho at Corinthians — one-word Brazilian full-back name.

players.name: Memphis Depay
players.quickFact: Netherlands forward whose skill and star power made him a marquee Corinthians signing in Série A.
players.playingStyle: Direct forward who dribbles from wide areas, shoots with power, and links with physical presence.
quizHints: Netherlands international forward who played for Lyon and Barcelona.
Corinthians star in black and white with flair and a strong left foot.
Memphis Depay — first name Memphis, not Dutch keeper names.

players.name: Yuri Alberto
players.quickFact: Brazil striker and reliable Corinthians goal threat after developing at Internacional and other Série A sides.
players.playingStyle: Centre-forward who attacks space, holds up when needed, and finishes with either foot.
quizHints: Brazil centre-forward in Corinthians black and white.
Mobile striker known for runs in behind and box movement.
Yuri Alberto — full name, not Yuri of other clubs.

players.name: Bruno Henrique
players.quickFact: Brazil winger who starred in Flamengo’s 2019 Libertadores run with pace, goals, and big-match mentality.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who attacks full-backs, cuts inside to shoot, and thrives in transition.
quizHints: Brazil international winger famous for Flamengo’s 2019 Copa Libertadores triumph.
Right-sided attacker in red and black known for speed and finishing.
Bruno Henrique at Flamengo — full name disambiguates from other Brunos in Brazil.

players.name: Erick Pulgar
players.quickFact: Chile defensive midfielder who captained Universidad de Chile before Fiorentina and Flamengo, anchoring La Roja in the middle.
players.playingStyle: Screening midfielder who tackles, intercepts, and distributes short to maintain shape.
quizHints: Chile holding midfielder in red who played for Fiorentina in Serie A.
Flamengo anchor — not Felipe Mora the forward.
Erick who sits deep and breaks up opposition attacks.

players.name: Giorgian de Arrascaeta
players.quickFact: Uruguay playmaker and Flamengo fan favourite — creativity, set pieces, and Libertadores nights at the Maracanã.
players.playingStyle: Technical number 10 who drifts between lines, delivers through-balls, and shoots with curl from distance.
quizHints: Uruguay international in red and black for Flamengo.
Creative number 10 with left-footed passes and Copa Libertadores pedigree.
Giorgian de Arrascaeta — not the Colombian striker with a similar first name.

players.name: Lucas Paquetá
players.quickFact: Brazil midfielder and 2022 World Cup squad member who returned to Flamengo after Lyon and West Ham.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who carries the ball, links play, and mixes skill with aggressive pressing.
quizHints: Brazil international midfielder in Flamengo red and black.
Box-to-box flair player known for skill, physical duels, and cards.
Lucas Paquetá at Flamengo — surname Paquetá in hints.

players.name: Pedro
players.quickFact: Brazil striker with Chelsea and Roma medals who leads the line for Flamengo with movement and composure.
players.playingStyle: Intelligent centre-forward who drops to combine, attacks space behind defenders, and finishes calmly.
quizHints: Former Chelsea and Roma centre-forward at Flamengo.
Brazil no. 9 type who links play and finishes in the box.
Flamengo striker Pedro — not Pedri of Barcelona or Pedro Neto.

players.name: Rafael Borré
players.quickFact: Colombia striker who won the Europa League with Eintracht Frankfurt before Brazil’s Serie A and leads the line for Los Cafeteros.
players.playingStyle: Target forward who links lay-offs, presses defenders, and finishes close-range chances with either foot.
quizHints: Colombia centre-forward in yellow who won Frankfurt’s Europa League.
Stocky striker at Internacional — not James Rodríguez the playmaker.
Rafael Borré known for hold-up play and penalty-box finishing.

players.name: Andreas Pereira
players.quickFact: Brazil midfielder with Manchester United academy roots who became a creative outlet for Palmeiras in Série A.
players.playingStyle: Creative midfielder who shoots from distance, switches play, and combines in the final third.
quizHints: Brazil playmaker who came through Manchester United before Palmeiras.
Left-footed midfielder known for shots and set-piece delivery.
Andreas Pereira at Palmeiras — not Andreas from other Brazilian clubs.

players.name: Felipe Anderson
players.quickFact: Brazil winger who starred for Lazio in Serie A before returning to Palmeiras with dribbling and engine on the right.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who dribbles, crosses, and tracks back to help the full-back.
quizHints: Brazil international winger who played for Lazio over many seasons.
Experienced wide player in Palmeiras green and white.
Felipe Anderson — full name avoids generic “Anderson” quiz clashes.

players.name: Gustavo Gómez
players.quickFact: Paraguay centre-back and Palmeiras captain who anchors title and Libertadores defences in green and white.
players.playingStyle: Commanding centre-back who wins aerial duels, steps out to intercept, and organises the line.
quizHints: Paraguay international who captains Palmeiras.
Tall centre-back known for headers, leadership, and aerial duels.
Gustavo Gómez at Palmeiras — not Facundo Medina of Marseille.

players.name: Vitor Roque
players.quickFact: Brazil forward who broke through at Palmeiras before a high-profile move toward European football.
players.playingStyle: Pacey forward who attacks channels, presses defenders, and finishes quickly in the box.
quizHints: Young Brazil striker who developed at Palmeiras.
Centre-forward linked with Barcelona in the early 2020s.
Vitor Roque — first name Vitor, surname Roque, at Palmeiras.

players.name: Gabriel Barbosa
players.quickFact: Brazil forward nicknamed Gabigol who won Libertadores titles with Flamengo after rising through Santos.
players.playingStyle: Poacher who finds space in the box, finishes first-time, and thrives on through-balls.
quizHints: Brazil striker nicknamed Gabigol with Santos and Flamengo success.
Santos product known for poaching and big-game goals.
Gabriel Barbosa at Santos — nickname Gabigol in hints, not Gabriel from Arsenal.

players.name: Neymar
players.quickFact: Brazil icon who developed at Santos before global stardom and a homecoming to the club in this dataset.
players.playingStyle: Skillful forward who dribbles from deep, draws fouls, and creates with flair and vision.
quizHints: Brazil’s generational no. 10 who won Olympic gold in 2016.
Santos academy graduate famous for skill, flair, and samba style.
Neymar at Santos in FootyBrain — not Neymar’s PSG era club in hints.

players.name: Rony
players.quickFact: Brazil winger who won trophies with Palmeiras and became a direct, pacey outlet for Santos.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who drives at defenders, cuts inside to shoot, and presses aggressively.
quizHints: Brazil winger known for speed and dribbling at Santos.
Former Palmeiras attacker with a powerful left foot.
Rony at Santos — one-name winger, not Rony of other leagues without club context.

players.name: Zé Rafael
players.quickFact: Brazil midfielder who became a Santos engine with tackling, carrying, and Série A experience.
players.playingStyle: Hard-working midfielder who presses, recovers possession, and progresses with simple passing.
quizHints: Brazil international midfielder in Santos white.
Box-to-box player known for energy and ball-winning.
Zé Rafael at Santos — not Rafael the São Paulo goalkeeper.

players.name: Jonathan Calleri
players.quickFact: Argentina striker famous for a Boca Libertadores explosion who became São Paulo’s penalty-box finisher.
players.playingStyle: Classic number 9 who attacks crosses, finishes inside the six-yard box, and holds up play.
quizHints: Argentina centre-forward who scored heavily for Boca Juniors.
São Paulo striker in white, red, and black known for poaching.
Jonathan Calleri — full hyphenated surname at São Paulo.

players.name: Luciano
players.quickFact: Brazil forward who scored consistently for São Paulo with movement, finishing, and versatility up front.
players.playingStyle: Mobile forward who drifts wide, combines quickly, and finishes with sharp movement in the box.
quizHints: Brazil attacker who starred for São Paulo in the Tricolor.
Forward who can play wide or through the middle.
São Paulo striker Luciano — not Luciano Acosta of MLS.

players.name: Marcos Antônio
players.quickFact: Brazil midfielder who brings energy, passing, and Liga MX experience to São Paulo’s midfield.
players.playingStyle: Energetic midfielder who presses, carries past lines, and plays vertical passes.
quizHints: Brazil central midfielder in São Paulo white, red, and black.
Box-to-box player known for carrying and combining in midfield.
Marcos Antônio — full name with accent, not Marcos of other teams.

players.name: Rafael
players.quickFact: Experienced Brazil goalkeeper who won titles with Atlético Mineiro and became a São Paulo senior stopper.
players.playingStyle: Composed goalkeeper who saves one-on-ones, commands the area, and distributes with both feet.
quizHints: Brazil goalkeeper in São Paulo kit — not Rafael of Spain at club level here.
Veteran keeper known for reflex saves and big-club experience.
São Paulo goalkeeper Rafael — one name, club context in hints.
